grounding_note: Expansion batch: more subject-matter disclosures behind 'protect my work' openings.

scenarios.opening_query: My food truck's whole identity got copied by a new competitor across town, right down to the details.
scenarios.final: Intellectual Property > Patent (Reg Patent Attys Only)
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Intellectual Property > Trademark/Copyright || Intellectual Property > Patent (Reg Patent Attys Only)
scenarios.hidden_fact: The detail that matters is my custom folding service-window mechanism — I have a provisional patent on it expiring in two months, and they copied the mechanism itself.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: What matters is my folding service-window mechanism — my provisional patent expires in two months and they copied the mechanism.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the copied element is a patented functional design
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's the name, colors, and menu art they took — pure branding.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: They took the name, colors, and menu art — pure branding.
scenarios.vetting_note: Provisional-deadline urgency; functional vs brand copying.

scenarios.opening_query: A big company used my drone photos in their national ad campaign without asking me.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Intellectual Property > Trademark/Copyright || Business and Corporate > General (contracts, entities)
scenarios.hidden_fact: There's a twist — I shot those under a contract with their marketing agency, and the whole fight is what usage rights that contract actually granted.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: I shot those under contract with their agency — the fight is what usage rights that contract granted.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a licensing contract governs the use
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No contract with anyone — they scraped them from my portfolio site; registrations are on file.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No contract — they scraped my portfolio site, and my registrations are on file.

scenarios.opening_query: Someone in another state started selling merch under my podcast's name and my listeners are buying it.
scenarios.final: Intellectual Property > Computer Law & Internet Law
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Intellectual Property > Trademark/Copyright || Intellectual Property > Computer Law & Internet Law
scenarios.hidden_fact: They also hijacked my podcast feed — redirected my RSS and locked me out of my own hosting account. Recovering the feed and account is the emergency.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They hijacked my actual feed — redirected my RSS and locked me out of hosting. Getting that back is the emergency.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether account/feed hijacking is the operative harm
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: My feed and accounts are fine — it's purely the name and logo on their merch.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: My accounts are fine — it's the name and logo on their merch.
scenarios.vetting_note: Platform-hijack facts select internet law over mark enforcement.

scenarios.opening_query: I developed a training method that a former student is now teaching commercially as her own.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Intellectual Property > Trademark/Copyright || Business and Corporate > General (contracts, entities) || General Litigation > General Litigation
scenarios.hidden_fact: She signed my instructor agreement with a two-year non-compete and confidentiality clause — enforcing that contract is the real case, since methods themselves aren't copyrightable.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: She signed my instructor agreement — two-year non-compete and confidentiality. That contract is the case, since methods aren't copyrightable.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a signed agreement provides the enforceable right
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No agreement — but she's republishing my copyrighted manuals and videos nearly verbatim under her name.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No agreement — but she republishes my copyrighted manuals and videos nearly verbatim.
scenarios.vetting_note: Idea-vs-expression trap; contract existence supplies the claim.

scenarios.opening_query: Our band's old manager registered things behind our backs and now claims he owns our name.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Intellectual Property > Trademark/Copyright || Business and Corporate > Entertainment
scenarios.hidden_fact: Our management contract has an ownership clause he's relying on — voiding that agreement (signed when two of us were minors) is the fight before any trademark question.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: His claim rests on the management contract's ownership clause — voiding that deal, signed when two of us were minors, comes before any trademark fight.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the contract's validity is the gateway issue
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No contract clause covers the name — he just filed a trademark application on it opportunistically, and we need it opposed.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No clause covers the name — he just filed a trademark on it, and we need that opposed.
scenarios.vetting_note: Row 126's entertainment-contract vs IP boundary with a minors-voidability hook.

scenarios.opening_query: I need a patent lawyer — a factory in another country is making my product.
scenarios.initial: Intellectual Property > Patent (Reg Patent Attys Only)
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Intellectual Property > Patent (Reg Patent Attys Only) || International Law > Customs Quotas & Duties
scenarios.hidden_fact: My patent's solid — the practical fight is stopping the knockoffs at the border: customs exclusion and import enforcement are what I actually need.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The patent's solid — what I need is border enforcement: customs exclusion to stop the imports.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether import/customs enforcement is the operative remedy
scenarios.counterfactual_final: Intellectual Property > Patent (Reg Patent Attys Only)
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The problem is my patent itself — their filings claim priority over mine and I need the patent fight handled.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The problem is the patent itself — they claim priority over my filing.
scenarios.vetting_note: Enforcement-mechanism disclosure: USPTO practice vs customs remedies.
